content: The orthographic and lexical origin of 'reflexion' as the older, variant spelling of the common term 'reflection' (taught in high school English vocabulary) can be used as a metaphor to frame a flaw in current LLM self-reflection mechanisms: most LLM self-reflection only revises responses from one consistent perspective, rather than embracing 'variant perspectives' analogous to variant spellings of the same core concept. This framing leads to a testable hypothesis that adding diverse variant response perspectives during LLM self-reflection, mirroring the linguistic variation captured by the spelling 'reflexion', improves downstream reasoning performance over single-perspective reflection.
